questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The Earth's atmosphere is composed of several layers. Q: The troposphere is the lowest layer where weather occurs. R: Above it lies the stratosphere containing the ozone layer. S: The mesosphere and thermosphere extend further upward.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: mining / India / in / industry / is / a / important / sector
options: An important sector in India is mining industry
Mining industry is a important sector in India
Mining is an important sector in India
In India mining is important sector
questions.solution: Mining is an important sector in India

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Varactor diode acts as:
questions.solution: Varactor: reverse-biased PN junction. C_j decreases with reverse voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bipolar junction transistor: current gain beta = Ic/Ib. Typical beta for small signal BJT:
questions.solution: Beta (hFE): varies with IC, temperature. Typical: 100-300 for small signal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8085 instruction cycle has:
options: Fetch, decode, execute (and sometimes memory read/write)
questions.solution: Machine cycle: fetch (T1-T4), then decode/execute. Each instruction: 1-6 machine cycles.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Interleaved boost converter advantages:
options: Reduced input current ripple and higher power density
questions.solution: Interleaved: multiple phases shifted. Ripple cancellation. Better thermal distribution. Higher power handling.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: FIR filter designed by window method: Gibbs phenomenon observed as:
questions.solution: Gibbs: truncating ideal IR gives ripples near discontinuities (band edges). Fixed amplitude, not reduced by more taps.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Smith chart: constant VSWR circles are:
questions.solution: VSWR = const circles are centered at Smith chart center. Higher VSWR → larger radius. Point on real axis gives VSWR value.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: I²C bus: uses how many wires for communication:
questions.solution: I²C: SDA (data) + SCL (clock). Open-drain with pull-ups. Multi-master, multi-slave. 100kHz standard, 400kHz fast.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Schering bridge: used to measure:
questions.solution: Schering bridge: measures C and tanδ. Used for cable insulation testing, capacitor characterization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An inverting amplifier gain is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Source coding — Huffman:
options: Variable-length code. Prefix code (no codeword is prefix of another). Optimal for given probabilities
questions.solution: Huffman: symbols sorted by probability. Short codes for frequent symbols. Entropy bound.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A BJT with β = 150 has a base current of 10 µA. Its collector current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: AM modulation index:
options: m = (V_max - V_min)/(V_max + V_min) or V_m/V_c. m ≤ 1 for no distortion

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The JFET is a:
questions.solution: JFET: V_GS controls channel width. I_D = I_DSS(1 - V_GS/V_p)². Pinch-off when V_GS = V_p.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PCM SNR = 6.02n + 1.76 dB. For 12-bit PCM:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BJT Darlington pair current gain:
questions.solution: Darlington: β_total = β₁×β₂. Very high gain but lower bandwidth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multiple access — OFDMA:
options: OFDM with subcarrier allocation to users. Scalable. LTE/5G uplink + downlink
questions.solution: OFDMA: subcarrier groups. SC-FDMA (LTE uplink): lower PAPR. Resource blocks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Zener diode operates in:
questions.solution: Zener: designed to operate in reverse breakdown without damage. V_Z ≈ 2-200 V.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: State variable feedback: pole placement requires:
questions.solution: Pole placement: place closed-loop poles arbitrarily via state feedback. Only possible if system is controllable.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A BJT with β = 100 has a base current of 20 µA. Its collector current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Closed-loop transfer function:
questions.solution: CLTF = forward/(1+loop gain). H(s)=1 for unity feedback. Sensitivity reduced by (1+GH).

questions.stem: LED forward voltage: typically 1.8V (red) to 3.3V (blue/white). For 5V supply and 20mA:

questions.stem: The transfer function is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Op-amp — Schmitt trigger:
options: Comparator with hysteresis. V_UT = V_ref·R2/(R1+R2) + V_sat·R1/(R1+R2)
questions.solution: Schmitt: positive feedback. V_HYS = 2·V_sat·R1/(R1+R2). Noise immunity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MOSFET threshold voltage V_TH for enhancement mode NMOS is:
questions.solution: Enhancement NMOS: V_TH > 0. Depletion NMOS: V_TH < 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Second-order system: ω_n=15 rad/s, ζ=0.2. Percentage overshoot ≈

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The impulse response h(t) of an LTI system:
questions.solution: LTI: y(t) = x(t)*h(t) = ∫x(τ)h(t-τ)dτ. Convolution in time = multiplication in freq domain.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The free-space wavelength of a 300 MHz signal is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 8-bit ADC has a full-scale range of 10 V. Its resolution (LSB step) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quarter-wave transformer:
options: Matching technique: Z_in = Z₀²/Z_L. Length = λ/4
questions.solution: Impedance transformation: Z_in = Z₀²/Z_L. Used for narrowband matching. Broadband: multi-section.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 4-to-1 multiplexer has:
questions.solution: 2ⁿ inputs → n select lines. 4=2² → 2 select lines.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multiple access — FDMA:
options: Each user assigned different frequency. Guard bands. Analog cellular (AMPS)
questions.solution: FDMA: simple but inefficient. GSM uses FDMA + TDMA. Guard bands (10-20% overhead).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The AM waveform's envelope (shown) has Amax = 10 V and Amin = 6 V. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The AM waveform's envelope (shown) has Amax = 15 V and Amin = 4 V. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A class A amplifier has conduction angle:
questions.solution: Class A: 360° (always on, low efficiency max 25%). Class B: 180° (push-pull, 78.5%).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Open-loop vs closed-loop:
options: Closed-loop uses feedback (compares output to reference)
questions.solution: Closed-loop: more accurate, stable, less sensitive to disturbances. Open-loop: simpler, no stability issues.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FIFO buffer: write pointer and read pointer. When pointers are equal:
options: Buffer is empty (or full, depending on implementation)
questions.solution: FIFO: write pointer advances on write, read pointer on read. Empty when equal. Full when one wraps.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An FM signal has peak deviation 5 kHz and message bandwidth 3 kHz. By Carson's rule the bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The AM waveform's envelope (shown) has Amax = 12 V and Amin = 2 V. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Huffman coding: average code length is:
questions.solution: Huffman: optimal prefix code. H(X) <= L_avg < H(X)+1. Achieves entropy in limit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A channel of bandwidth 4 kHz has an SNR of 30 dB. Its Shannon capacity is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Entropy H(X) of a binary source with P(0)=0.75:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Beamforming:
options: Phased array. Adjust phase of each element. Steer beam direction. 5G massive MIMO
questions.solution: Beamforming: digital, analog, hybrid. Narrow beam → more users. 3GPP beam management.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: OFDM:
options: Orthogonal frequency division multiplexing. Multiple subcarriers, cyclic prefix. Used in 4G/5G, WiFi
questions.solution: OFDM: FFT-based. Subcarrier orthogonality. CP eliminates ISI. PAPR issue. LTE: SC-FDMA uplink.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PCM SNR for 16-bit quantization:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM signal carries a message of highest frequency 5 kHz. Its transmission bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Channel capacity — Shannon-Hartley:
options: C = B·log₂(1 + S/N) bps. Approaches entropy limit
questions.solution: Shannon: C increases linearly with B, logarithmically with SNR. MIMO multiplies capacity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An FM signal has peak deviation 75 kHz and message bandwidth 3 kHz. By Carson's rule the bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An FM signal has peak deviation 15 kHz and message bandwidth 5 kHz. By Carson's rule the bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Adaptive delta modulation (ADM):
options: Adjusts step size based on slope (reduces slope overload and granular noise)
questions.solution: ADM: step size increases with consecutive same-direction bits, decreases with alternation.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Class-D audio amplifier output filter: purpose:
questions.solution: LC low-pass filter reconstructs audio from PWM. Removes high-frequency switching artifacts while passing audio band.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spread spectrum — DSSS:
options: Direct sequence. XOR data with PN code. Processing gain = B_rf/B_data
questions.solution: DSSS: CDMA. PN code: maximal length sequence. Near-far problem. Multipath rejection.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Modulation — need for:
options: Frequency translation, antenna size (λ/4), multiplexing, noise immunity
questions.solution: Modulation: shift baseband to carrier frequency. Practical antenna size ∝ λ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an inverting op-amp circuit, the inverting input behaves as a:
questions.solution: Negative feedback forces the inverting input to ≈ 0 V (virtual ground) when the + input is grounded.

questions.stem: Phase shift oscillator requires 180° phase shift from 3 RC sections. Each section provides:
questions.solution: 3 RC sections: each 60° at oscillation frequency. Total 180°. Op-amp provides additional 180°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnitude response shown is flat then rolls off at −20 dB/decade past f_c ≈ 100 Hz. This is the response of a:
questions.solution: Constant gain at low frequencies with a single −20 dB/decade roll-off above f_c ≈ 100 Hz is a first-order low-pass response.

questions.stem: If two amplifier stages of 20 dB and 30 dB are cascaded, the overall gain is:
questions.solution: Gains in dB add: 20 + 30 = 50 dB (i.e. voltage gains multiply).

questions.stem: Which statement about negative feedback is INCORRECT?
questions.solution: Negative feedback reduces gain (in exchange for bandwidth, linearity and stability).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Barkhausen criterion for oscillation:
questions.solution: Loop gain = 1 at 0° phase. For start-up: |Aβ| > 1. Steady-state: |Aβ| = 1.

questions.stem: The Routh–Hurwitz criterion is used to determine system stability without:
questions.solution: Routh's array tests sign changes in the first column to count right-half-plane roots directly.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Cyclic redundancy check (CRC): detects:
options: Burst errors up to polynomial degree
questions.solution: CRC: polynomial division remainder. Detects all burst errors ≤ degree, all single-bit errors. Strong error detection.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An n-channel enhancement MOSFET enters the saturation (active) region when:
options: V_GS > V_th and V_DS ≥ V_GS − V_th
questions.solution: Above threshold and with V_DS pinching off the channel, the MOSFET saturates and acts as a current source.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Interleaving:
options: Reorders bits to spread burst errors. Block or convolutional. Used with FEC
questions.solution: Interleaver: scrambles bits. Depth = rows. Burst → random errors after deinterleaving. FEC corrects.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 9 kHz tone is sampled at 10 kHz. The alias frequency that appears in the baseband is:
questions.solution: Alias = |f − fs| = |9 − 10| = 1 kHz (since f exceeds fs).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Error control — Hamming codes:
options: Single error correction. (7,4) code: 3 parity bits. d_min = 3
questions.solution: Hamming: (7,4), (15,11), (31,26). Parity check matrix H. Syndrome decoding.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Convolution of x(t) with δ(t-T):
questions.solution: sifting property: x(t)*δ(t-T) = x(t-T). Shifts signal by T.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Op-amp — input bias current:
options: DC current into input terminals. I_b = (I_+ + I_-)/2. For BJT: μA. For FET: pA
questions.solution: I_b: causes offset voltage due to source resistance. Compensate with equal R on both inputs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mod-n counter:
options: Counts 0 to n-1 then resets. Decade counter (0-9) = mod-10
questions.solution: Mod: truncate natural count. Clear or preset. 74LS90 (decade), 74LS93 (4-bit).
